name: Dynamic
what_it_does: Provides a Web3-native authentication and user management platform supporting embedded wallets, social login, wallet sign-in (SIWE), and multi-chain identity via pre-built UI components and a REST API.
use_cases: Add crypto-native authentication (Sign-In With Ethereum) alongside social login (Google, Twitter, Discord) in a single auth flow
Provision embedded wallets for users who don't have a crypto wallet, enabling non-custodial onboarding without MetaMask
Manage user identity across multiple wallets and linked social accounts from a unified backend API
Gate features or content based on on-chain attributes (token holdings, NFT ownership) verified at login
Build multi-tenant Web3 apps with per-environment API keys and isolated user bases
not_for: Pure Web2 apps with no crypto or blockchain component — simpler auth providers (Auth0, Clerk) will be less expensive and easier to integrate
Backend services that need programmatic wallet creation at scale without a user-facing authentication flow
best_when: Best when building Web3 consumer apps that need to onboard both crypto-native users (via wallet) and mainstream users (via social login) with minimal friction and a polished UI.
avoid_when: Avoid when your authentication requirements are purely traditional (username/password, SSO) with no blockchain identity component, as the pricing and complexity are not justified.

auth.notes: Environment API keys for server-side REST calls. Client-side SDK uses publishable key. JWTs issued on successful auth can be verified server-side using Dynamic's JWKS endpoint.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 5,000 monthly active users on free tier with full feature access
pricing.paid_tiers: Growth from $499/month; Enterprise with custom pricing and SLA
pricing.notes: Significant price jump from free to paid; suitable for projects expecting to stay under 5k MAU or with budget for Growth tier.

performance.rate_limits: REST API rate limits apply per environment key; specific thresholds available in dashboard

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors include structured JSON with code and message fields. SDK errors surface as typed exceptions in TypeScript, which aids agent-side error classification.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation is idempotent by wallet address or email. Wallet linking operations should be checked for existing state before calling to avoid duplicate association errors.
known_agent_gotchas: JWT verification requires fetching Dynamic's JWKS endpoint; agents should cache the JWKS and handle key rotation without breaking auth flows
Embedded wallet provisioning is asynchronous — agents polling for wallet readiness must implement backoff rather than immediate re-query
Domain allowlist must include all origins making SDK calls; misconfiguration blocks auth silently in some browsers

security_score_components.security_notes: Strong security posture: JWKS-based JWT verification, separate publishable vs secret keys, domain allowlisting, SOC2 Type II certified. SIWE implementation follows EIP-4361 spec.
